conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child - my son Alex. He's 6 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex.

Now I need to ask you some questions to determine eligibility. 

Are you and Alex currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

Next question: Are you responsible for Alex in terms of providing for his care and upbringing? This typically means things like providing him with a home, food, clothing, and looking after his day-to-day needs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm fully responsible for him. I provide his home, food, clothes, and look after all his daily needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Next, I need to ask: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

One more important question: Do you or your partner (if you have one) have an individual income over £50,000 per year? This is relevant because there's a High Income Child Benefit Charge that may apply.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into those details right now - I'm just trying to find out if I'm eligible first. Can we focus on that?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Of course, I understand. The income question is actually more about a potential tax charge rather than eligibility itself, so we can set that aside for now.

Just to confirm a few final details: Is Alex a British citizen or does he have settled status in the UK? Or if not, what is his immigration status?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Just one more question: Are there any special circumstances I should know about? For example, is Alex in any form of care (such as local authority care), or are there any custody arrangements or other situations that might affect who is responsible for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually, Alex was in care for a short period recently - just a couple of months. But he's back home with me now and living with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. That's helpful to know.

Just to clarify: Is Alex currently still in any form of local authority care, or is he now fully back in your care with no ongoing care arrangements with the local authority?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's fully back in my care now. There are no ongoing care arrangements - he's just living at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for clarifying that.

Just to make sure I have all the information I need: Is there anything else about your or Alex's situation that might be relevant? For example:
- Is Alex in full-time non-advanced education or approved training (though at age 6 he would typically be in primary school)?
- Are there any other unusual circumstances I should be aware of?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing unusual. He's just in primary school like any other 6-year-old. Everything's pretty straightforward now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the key eligibility criteria:

✓ Alex is under 16 years old (he's 6)
✓ You both live in the UK
✓ You are responsible for Alex's care and upbringing
✓ Alex is a British citizen
✓ He is not in local authority care (he's living with you full-time)
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. 

Just as a note for your awareness: while you are eligible to claim, if you or your partner have an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay a High Income Child Benefit Charge through your tax return. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim and receive the benefit, you would just need to pay back some or all of it through tax. You can always claim but opt not to receive payments if that suits your situation better.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) He is under 16 years old (he is 6 years old), 2) He lives in the UK with the claimant in Manchester, 3) The claimant is responsible for Alex, providing his home, food, clothes and day-to-day care, 4) Alex is a British citizen, 5) He is not currently in local authority care (he was previously in care for a short period but is now fully back in the claimant's care), 6) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the necessary conditions for Child Benefit eligibility are satisfied.

All the necessary conditions for Child Benefit eligibility are satisfied.
